title: ericameria nauseosa var. bernardina
intro: Ericameria nauseosa var. bernardina, also known as San Bernardino rubber rabbitbrush, is a shrub-like plant species within the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Ericameria nauseosa var. bernardina include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which make it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including colds, sore throats, and respiratory problems, as well as to soothe wounds and reduce fever. Ericameria nauseosa var. bernardina can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves and stems are often used in herbal remedies, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
